description: §38.12 — an `export <channel name=@topic>` whose `name=` is a reactive-ref (not a string literal) fires E-CHANNEL-EXPORT-001. A cross-file channel export's wire identity must be compile-time stable, so a `name=@var` form is rejected. (The reactive-ref also raises an incidental E-SCOPE-001 on the unresolved `@topic` after the export is fail-open dropped; the codes-half superset check pins only E-CHANNEL-EXPORT-001.)
rationale: §38.12: `export <channel ...>` SHALL carry a string-literal `name="..."` — reactive-ref / interpolated forms are unsupported because the wire-layer identity must be compile-time stable. A reactive-ref `name=` on an exported channel SHALL emit E-CHANNEL-EXPORT-001 (P3.A).
